Notice Of Motion And Petitioners Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Mar-10-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MAX FOLKENFLIK OF THE FOLKENFLIK & MCGERITY LLP, TEL. NO. (212) 757-0400 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. SUSAN J. HARRIMAN OF THE KEKER & VAN NEST LLP, TEL. NO. 676-2213 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; Petitioner David Kent's Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award - The petition to vacate is denied and the court confirms the arbitration award. The 8/15/14 letter does not clearly object to the timeliness of the final award. The parties agreed that the arbitrator would still decide the prevailing party issue and the gist of that letter is that the interim award is the final award. Petitioner has waived the issue per CCP 1283.8. Petitioner's other arguments also lack merit. The interim award was interim and an arbitrator is allowed to adjudicate the case in an incremental manner. The arbitrator had the authority to determine the prevailing party. Petitioner is basically arguing a legal error, which is not reviewable. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

Off Calendar Mar-10-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

ATTY. SUSAN J. HARRIMAN OF THE KEKER & VAN NEST LLP, TEL. NO. 676-2213 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Petitioner David Kent's Motion To Strike is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

Off Calendar Mar-10-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MAX FOLKENFLIK OF THE FOLKENFLIK & MCGERITY LLP, TEL. NO. (212) 757-0400 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. SUSAN J. HARRIMAN OF THE KEKER & VAN NEST LLP, TEL. NO. 676-2213 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENTS THE WINE GROUP LLC, TWG MANAGER, INC., ART CIOCCA, H JESSE, RICHARD MAHONEY AND BRIAN VOS' Hearing On Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award; Request To Confirm Arbitration Award Pursuant To CCP 167; 1285.2 - The petition to vacate is denied and the court confirms the arbitration award. The 8/15/14 letter does not clearly object to the timeliness of the final award. The parties agreed that the arbitrator would still decide the prevailing party issue and the gist of that letter is that the interim award is the final award. Petitioner has waived the issue per CCP 1283.8. Petitioner's other arguments also lack merit. The interim award was interim and an arbitrator is allowed to adjudicate the case in an incremental manner. The arbitrator had the authority to determine the prevailing party. Petitioner is basically arguing a legal error, which is not reviewable. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
